title: Order the compounder's life-cycle
intro: The stages a wealth creator moves through — put them in order.
prompt: Drag into sequence, earliest stage first.

steps.text: Build — heavy investment; ROCE dips; looks expensive and dull
Moat forms — pricing power / switching costs hold; ROCE inflects upward
Compounds — earnings grow for years; profits reinvested into the core
Maturity — moat widening slows; complacency and challengers become the risk

debrief: Wealth is created in the compounding stage, but it's only reachable by surviving the unglamorous build phase (where impatient investors leave) and proving the moat. The final risk isn't collapse — it's complacency once the company is a large-cap incumbent.
